observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in analyzing the discrepancies noted in the SCBAA and in the reconciliation statement, and to prepare the corrected reports. Further, substantiate the reconciling items in the SCBAA by providing supporting schedules that can be verified against the amounts presented in the FS; and
ensure adequate disclosure, which should include narrative description and detailed analyses, in the NFS regarding the reconciling items in the SCBAA reconciliation statement.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the cash flow from operating activities using the indirect method in accordance with the provision of IPSAS 2 and disclose the reconciliation in the NFS to provide the users of FS sufficient information.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record transactions in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue and expenses set out in IPSASs and the RCA.

We recommended that the Management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to adjust the issued tangible items acquired in prior years, with cost below the capitalization threshold, and charge the same to Prior Period Adjustment and Government Equity, while tangible items that are still in the custody of the General Services Office (GSO), if any, shall be recorded to the appropriate semi-expendable property accounts; and
GSO and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 particularly on the procedures in the receipt, inspection, acceptance and recording of semi-expendable properties; and maintenance property records.

We recommended that the Management require the BAC to:
update and revise the RFQ form used in all procurements undertaken thru alternative methods to indicate PhilGEPs Registration Number, ABC, and other terms and conditions of the contract; and
require the suppliers to indicate the brand of goods they are offering and other necessary information that may affect the final evaluation of the offers.

We recommended that the Management establish a clear internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of employees to ensure fair compensation and maintaining employee morale while also promoting efficiency and transparency in accordance with CSC and DBM JC No. 02, s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the GSO designate to take appropriate action in maintaining Property Cards for all PPE of the LGU;
direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and proper Property Ledger Cards and reconcile them accordingly with the records of the GSO; and
create an Inventory Committee for the sole purpose of conducting the one-time cleansing of the Municipality's PPE and direct the said committee to immediately begin the process of complying with it in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We further recommended that upon reorganization of the Inventory Committee, Management ensure that a physical count of all properties will be conducted at least once a year and   prepare the RPCPPE to be submitted to the audit team.

We recommended that the LCE:
require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare and formulate, in a separate caption, disaster risk and reduction management PPAs that will be funded from the unexpended balances under the Trust Liabilities account to be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP. Ensure that the fund source of each PPA is indicated (i.e., CY 2023 Budget or Trust Liabilities);
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the MDRRMO to revise the Annual Investment Plan to include the disaster risk reduction and management PPAs to be charged from the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of the previous years subject to the approval of the LCE and Local Sanggunian, which will serve as the basis in the utilization thereof; and
require the MDRRMO to optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.
We further recommended that the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRM Plan to present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum.

We recommended that the Management:
ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF by implementing the PPAs to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and developmental outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents; and
identify those fully completed PPAs with unutilized balances and PPAs that could not be implemented, and reprogram the same to other social, economic, developmental or environmental PPAs.

We recommended that the Management implement those projects and programs under its continuing appropriations for the previous years and ensure all programs included in its capital expenditure are carried out to achieve the intended socio-economic goals by:
ensuring that all programs and projects under capital outlay are implemented by the concerned department or office for the calendar year to which they were appropriated,
examining the projects included in the CYs 2015-2023 and determine those which are still necessary to implement and when identified projects could no longer be implemented, not viable, not feasible for implementation, no longer needed, reprogram the same for more viable projects or programs of equal importance for the current or succeeding years,
monitoring the procurement schedule prepared by each department at least quarterly and when determined to be untimely, notify those concerned departments and direct them to implement the same.
Moreover, we recommended that the savings realized from the implemented projects be appropriated, by ordinance, and be made available for other priority projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management immediately facilitate the integration of barangay clearance collection in the Municipality’s collection system in order to expedite the processing of application and renewal of permits of business owners in the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the BAC to prepare a resolution declaring and justifying that direct retail purchase as the chosen mode of procurement for POL products and delegation of the procurement to specific officials, personnel or committee or office the conduct of such purchases. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor shall prepare and issue an issuance or an order for the same purpose;
Ensure that each office under the Municipality provide a necessary estimate of needed POL products within the fiscal year, including provisions for foreseeable emergencies based on historical records, which will be subjected to direct retail purchase and consolidated in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan; and
Make sure that direct purchases are made from any available retailers, dealers or gas stations at retail pump price and the requirement must be intended to be used for any official government vehicles and equipment for immediate consumption.

The BAC assured the Audit Team that they religiously abide with the recommendations enumerated above to properly support the validity of their purchases for POL.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the MA to comply with the disclosure requirements as stated in Paragraphs 29, and 59 to 62 of IPSAS 2, adequately providing information and computation on the net cash flow from operating activities using the indirect method, and the amount of restricted and unrestricted cash balance as at year-end to provide the FS users sufficient information about the Municipality’s cash and cash equivalents.

We recommended that Management initiate the passage of the ordinance that would provide the rules and regulations on the utilization of the CDF in order to enjoy the full benefits that could be derived from the implementation of programs and activities sourced from the said fund.

The Municipal Vice Mayor commented that the crafting of the IRR governing the disbursement of the said fund as required under Section 199 of the 2018 Municipal Revenue Code lies in the Executive Department. Nonetheless, the Local Chief Executive proposed that a development plan covering the CDF shall also be prepared along with the IRR and these will be forwarded to the Legislative Department for their concurrence.

We recommended that Management:
Whenever applicable, implement the PPAs listed above as embodied in the MDP;
Revisit the plans and budget embodied in the Municipal EDP and identify the necessities of the listed projects therein, otherwise identify more appropriate and responsive PPAs; and
Appropriate the unexpended balances derived from previous implementation of projects to new projects that are aligned to the purpose of the 20% DF. These would ensure that benefits intended for the Municipality’s populace will be met.

The Management commented that some of the listed PPAs above funded under the current and continuing appropriations for 20% DF have already been started with its implementation. Further, they told the Audit Team that they will consult the Provincial Planning and Development Office for possible re-appropriation of the unexpended balances derived from the implementation of previous projects funded under the same fund.

We further recommended, when applicable, to seek for disconnection of any existing line that are no longer necessary.

e. Prepare and submit monthly lapsing schedule on depreciation of PPE accounts.

restate the affected accounts for all ensuing FS, if there is any, in order to
fairly present the financial statements of the Municipality.

1.10 We further recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that all adjustments are properly documented in
order to achieve faithful representation of all transactions.

2.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the following proposed adjusting journal entries for the
proper accounting of the transaction in the financial statements:
a. General Fund

2.8 In addition, with the foregoing PAJEs, it is recommended that the
Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that
transactions recorded in the books of the Municipality are complete in
order to fairly present the balances of accounts in the financial
statements.

2.9 Lastly, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to transfer the amount of ₱4,100,000.00 from the Trust Fund
to the General Fund.

3.8 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate transfer of
the cash balance amounting to ₱3,693,894.98 to the TF depository bank
account and henceforth for all unexpended balances relating to QRF and
MOOE under the LDRRMF in accordance with Item 5.1.10 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

4.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to determine the cause of the variance between the net cash flow from
operating activities presented under the direct method and indirect
method.

4.8 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that
the cash flows statement of the Municipality is accurate so that users of
the FS could rely upon the amounts disclosed for better decision making.

5.8 We recommended that Management:
a. strictly observe the provisions of Sections 305 and 337 of RA No.
7160;
b. exercise prudence in the utilization of funds to eliminate cash
overdraft; and
c. require the Local Finance Committee and Municipal Accountant
to determine the underlying causes of the cash deficit in order to
properly address the condition.

6.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to identify the nature of the noted
discrepancies between their records and immediately effect the
necessary adjustments.

6.6 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer always reconcile their records at least quarterly to avoid
further accumulation of unreconciled items.

7.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to observe the disclosure requirements by various IPSAS provisions in
the preparation of the financial statements to meet the completeness of
information so that users of the FS could evaluate the significance of the
accounts.

8.5 We recommended that Management require the refund of the financial
assistance received by the ineligible beneficiaries and return the amount
collected to the DSWD FO1.

9.6 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to release the
shares on the Barangays RPT collections within five days after the end of
each quarter to enable the barangays to have additional funds for their
operations.

10.12 We recommended that Management demand the return of the excessive
amount from the responsible officials and employees who prepared and
reviewed the ABC and the concerned supplier of the six projects.

10.13 We further recommended that Management instruct the:
a. responsible employees to prepare all ABCs in accordance with the
prescribed rules and regulations of DPWH DO No. 197 s. 2016; and
b. Municipal Engineer to conduct a thorough review on the
computation of ABCs to have accurate information to be used in
the budgeting of Municipality’s expenditures.

11.5 We recommended that Management require the elective officials to
refund the excess of the RATA received by them.

11.6 We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant for the strict compliance to the provisions of the DBM LBC
No. 103.

12.4 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to
maintain a list of completed projects as a reference for posting the
required warranty securities

12.5 Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the contractors
to post Warranty Securities of completed infrastructure projects with
issued Certificates of Acceptance to guarantee that the they perform
their responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 of RA No. 9184.

13.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to
extend the performance security until the final acceptance of the project.

14.8 We recommended that Management immediately apply the remedies
available as provided in Section 8.5 of RA No. 9184 for the unjustified
delay in the completion of the project, which is, to rescind the contract,
forfeit the contractor’s performance security and takeover the
prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified contractor
through negotiated contract; and

14.9 We further recommended that Management require the contractor to
immediately pay the estimated liquidated damages.

15.9 We recommended that Management instruct the GSO – Designate to
ensure that all the motor vehicles owned by the Municipality are
promptly registered with LTO as government vehicles under the name
of the Municipality of Paoay.

15.10 Henceforth, we further recommended that Management instruct the
GSO- Designate expedite the renewal of registration with the LTO of all
serviceable motor vehicles in compliance with RA No. 4136 to avoid
penalties for late payment.

16.4 We recommended that Management, thru its Sanggunian Bayan, enact
an ordinance to localize the applicability and ensure the enforcement of
the Safe Spaces Act or RA No. 11313 and its IRR.

17.4 We recommended that Management instruct the heads of implementing
offices and the local finance committee to stop the practice of utilizing
the 20% Development Fund for purposes other than those incorporated
in the approved local development plan.

17.5 We further recommended that Management to cause the reversion of the
accumulated savings from prior years’ 20% DF completed projects and
the re-appropriation of the same to priority development
program/project to be determined by the MDC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
make a JEV to reclassify the account “Land” to “Investment Property- Land”, in
compliance to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that Management require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory in order
to present a realistic balance of property accounts and make a fair
presentation of accounts in the financial statements;
b. Municipal Accountant to:
 maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards;
 submit a complete and accurate Lapsing Schedule with
specific details of the PPE items for the Audit Team to
verify its correctness and include the date of acquisition
and estimated useful life of its PPE to properly present
its carrying amount;
 coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to analyze and
investigate the noted discrepancies on the Land account
and make the necessary adjustments as appropriate;
 coordinate with the GSO to gather the documents
necessary in updating or reconstructing their recor to be
able to provide depreciation to the subject PPE items in
item G. of the noted observations, and for the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of the
depreciation expense for the year, accumulated
depreciation for prior years and the corresponding
amount to the government equity account;
 reclassify to the proper Inventory account tangible assets
with estimated useful life of more than one year but small
enough to be considered and recorded as PPE.
Moreover, record the items as expense upon issuance of
the small tangible items; and
 prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
misclassification of PPE items mentioned in item h. of the
noted observations. Thereafter, ensure that all PPEs are
properly recorded based on the nature of the procured
items.
c. Property/Supply Officer to always maintain for each class of PPE a
Property Card for the recording of PPE with information as to its
description, custody and disposal; and
d. Acting GSO to:
 tender the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip to
the personnel to whom the items are issued for proper
monitoring, control and accountability;
 act on disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance
to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 89-
286.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
 immediately correct the accounting errors and henceforth
compute the depreciation in accordance with the provisions of
numbers 12 and 14 of Item IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008,
paragraph 59 of IPSAS 17 and item e.1 of DPWH DO No. 176 s.
2015;
 recognize the cost of labor of various projects and make
necessary adjusting entries to reduce the government equity
account and increase the road network account in accordance
to paragraph 30 of IPSAS 17;
 immediately coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and
Acting GSO for the completion of the inventory of roads and
bridges that were implemented and completed prior to CY 2016
in order to reinstate these in the books; and
 coordinate with the GSO for the reconciliation of their records
as regards Road Networks on or before the end of each year.
b. Municipal Engineer to periodically provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Acting GSO with the complete description and cost segregation
of the road components for road projects as basis of the latter to update
their respective records/inventory;
c. Municipal Assessor to immediately coordinate with the Municipal
Engineer for the valuation of the related road lots and provide the list
of values to the Municipal Accountant and Acting GSO for proper
recording thereof until the end of the third quarter of CY 2021 in
compliance with number 2 of Item IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008;
and
d. Municipal GSO to maintain and periodically update the Municipality
Government’s Local Road Inventory and Road Map and include the
value of the road lot components provided by the City Assessor.

We recommended that Management:
a. Cause the Inventory Committee to conduct the regular physical count
of inventories and furnish a copy of the RCPI to the Office of the
Auditor;
b. Require the Acting GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
to conduct periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records
and the immediate verification of discrepancies, if any, to effect the
necessary adjustment/s in the Municipality books; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 record all purchases of inventory items as inventory except
those paid out of the petty cash fund;

observations.managementAction: Management has minimized the reimbursement. Payments of expenditures were still made through reimbursement where extreme circumstances applied but procurement procedures were strengthened.
observations.reason: Some reimbursements remained but overall reduction observed; procurement planning improved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management take the following courses of action:

a. Undertake actual physical inventory of all properties of the municipal
government and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI duly
reconciled with the Property and Accounting records and submit
copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office for verification within the
reglementary period prescribed by existing rules and regulations as
herein discussed;

b. Require the GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to
prioritize and exert best efforts to reconcile their records as regards
properties/inventories to ensure the accuracy, existence, completeness
and reliability of the same in the financial statements;

c. Notify and/or inform the Office of the Auditor as to when inventory
taking will take place in order for us to witness the said activity; and

d. Employ effective internal control practice in managing the property,
plant and equipment of the municipality by using inventory/property
tags and renewing the Property Acknowledgement Receipts every
three (3) years after issuance.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO Designate and
Municipal Engineer to comply to the accounting and reporting
guidelines as prescribed in COA Circular 2015-008;

b. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local
roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a
Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and

c. Adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 dated
November 25, 2015.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with each other and reconcile the discrepancies on their
records. Adjust and/or correct the record that needs adjustment for
the fair presentation of the financial statements; and

b. Reconcile regularly or at least quarterly the cashbooks with that of
the corresponding subsidiary ledger in accordance with the provisions
of Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 6.3 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended and management agreed on the following:

a) Present supporting documents of recorded year-end payables to
establish their validity, propriety and existence;

b) Revisit the existing process of procurement of the municipal
government starting from planning, budgeting and accounting and
design an effective monitoring system with the objective of reflecting
the true amount of Accounts Payable duly supported by proper and
necessary documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayer’s names and the amount due
and collectible for each year as the basis for the Municipal Accountant to make a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record a more accurate RPT Receivable and
SEFT Receivable in order to fairly present the financial position of the
municipal government.

We recommended that:

a. All disbursements be supported by an appropriation made by law in
adherence to Section 305 (a) of R.A. 7160.

b. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015 which provides for the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units as prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector
Accounting Standards.

We recommended that concerned employees refund the overpayment.

We further recommended that management adhere strictly with the provisions
of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2012 on the proper computation of
Subsistence Allowance to prevent overpayment.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:

a. See to it that drugs and medicines being procured by the municipality
are obtained from authorized suppliers, distributors and/or
manufacturers as stated in their License to Operate and List of
Sources;

b. Ensure the authenticity, safety and effectiveness and quality of
procured drugs and medicines by requiring the supplier to submit
together with their bids and/or quotations Certificate of Product
Registration, Certificate of Good Manufacturing Practice and Batch
Release Certificates from Bureau of Foods and Drugs Administration;

c. Adherence to the provisions of Administrative Order No. 43 series
1999, Administrative Order No. 47-A series of 2001 dated August 30,
2001 and Administrative Order No. 103 series 2002 issued by the
Department of Health, Republic Act No. 3720 otherwise known as the
Foods, Drugs and Devices and Cosmetics Act as amended by Executive
Order No. 175, COA Circular 2012-001 and Presidential Decree No.
1445.

We recommended and management agreed to discontinue designating a
contractual employee as collector so that necessary bond can be secured from
the Bureau of Treasury as required by Section 305 (f) of the Local Government
Code.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to Treasury Circular
02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 and effect the increase of fidelity bonds of
accountable officers to amounts corresponding to their maximum cash
accountabilities in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates of
Fidelity Bond issued by the Bureau of Treasury, in order to provide adequate
protection on the resources of the municipal government.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the mandated use
of government plates and markings and have all motor vehicles owned by the
municipal government be marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” and the
name of the municipal government written below it to properly identify the
vehicles as government property in adherence to pertinent provisions of COA
Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.

We recommended that management support all claim vouchers for
infrastructure projects with pictures of the project, “before”, “during” and
“after” implementation, as required by RA No. 6954.

a) Comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 on the warranty security for
procurement of goods, and ensure adequate documentation as per
COA Circular 2012-001.

b) Ensure timely posting of Notices of Award in PhilGEPS as
required by RA 9184.

c) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly evaluate
the completeness of Bidding Documents, Eligibility and Technical
requirements and other documents before recommending the award
of contracts to the Municipal Mayor; the municipal government shall
enter into contract with the winning bidder, provided, that all
documentary requirements are complied with; and the Municipal
Accountant must require all pertinent documents before certifying
the disbursement vouchers as to the completeness of supporting
documents in line with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to avoid doubts on
the legality, validity and propriety of transactions.

(Additional recommendations related to other infractions under this
finding were included in the report.)

We recommended and management agreed to require the supplier to
submit its permit to sell radio transceivers and secure permit to possess from the
National Telecommunication Commission to avoid audit suspension.

We recommended and management agreed to adhere to the policies and
guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to
ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that management follow the guidelines in the granting,
utilization and accounting of the funds released to NGOs/POs provided under
COA Circular No. 2007-001 and require the submission of documents mentioned
in Items 2.1 and 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order that the
municipality be able to verify the implementation of the projects as agreed upon
and ensure that government funds be spent in accordance with the purposes for
which the same are granted.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal
Accountant prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF on a monthly basis in accordance with the procedures and
requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5 of said COA Circular.

We recommended and management agreed to comply with the afore-
cited regulations which require the maintenance of special accounts with the
complete subsidiary records for the transactions of the Springfield Memorial
Park as basis in the preparation of its financial statements which are essential
for decision-making and future reference and in pursuance to the provision of
Section 105-110 of the MNGAS.

We recommended management to:

a) Strictly and consistently observe the above-cited provisions of law,
rules, and regulations; and

b) Submit on time all the required reports and disbursement vouchers,
payrolls together with complete supporting documents to the COA
Auditor in order not to delay audit of transactions and preparation
of other reports.

We recommended that the municipal government:
a) Exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the municipality as required under Sec. 39 (2) of PD No. 1445 and Sec. 449 of the GAAM, Volume I; and

b) Proper coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor be made to reconcile discrepancies in their records as regards the Land account. Adjust and/or correct the record that needs adjustments to fairly present the said account in the financial statements.

We recommended management to:

a. Set-up and/or record accounts payable with valid and complete supporting documents in adherence to Section 46 of Presidential Decree No. 1177, Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and PPSAS 1.

b. Revisit the existing process of procurement of the Municipal government starting from planning, budgeting and accounting and design an effective monitoring system with the objective of reflecting the true amount of Accounts Payable duly supported by proper and necessary documents.

We recommended that management:

a) Apply effective cash programming to facilitate the proper handling of cash; 

b) Adopt austerity measures on disbursements to improve the cash position of the municipality; and

c) Observe strictly the provisions of Section 305(e) and Section 337 of Republic Act 7160.

We recommended management:

a) That lump-sum appropriations be supported with detailed and/or specific programs, projects and activities in order to achieve transparency as well as effective and efficient planning and budgeting; and

b) To observe strictly the provisions of Section 29(1), Article VI of the 1987 Constitution; Section 22 (c) of Republic Act No. 7160 and the Supreme Court ruling in Quisumbing, et al. vs. Garcia, G.R. No. 175527 as herein pointed out.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provision stated above. Collections for the day should be deposited intact with the authorized depository bank. Collections not deposited during the day due to cut-off time should be deposited in the next banking day to avoid risk from using the collections in paying obligations and/or exposing the same to possible loss or misappropriation.

We recommended the following:

a) That the Local Chief Executive instruct all concerned personnel to settle and/or liquidate their outstanding cash advances as of year-end; and

b) Enforce strictly the existing laws, rules and regulations governing cash advances to avoid abuse, possible loss and/or misuse of government funds.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure strict observance of the pertinent provisions of Section 7 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184 with a view of attaining efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its procurement opportunities.

We recommended the management to ensure strict observance of the provisions of Section 54.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976 in the procurement of goods with a view of attaining efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations.

We recommended management the following: 

a. Continue its drive on solid waste disposal by promoting the waste minimization programs and information dissemination on the proper waste segregation and proper disposal of the same in attaining cleanliness and keeping a pollution-free environment; and

b. Effective monitoring on the full implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the said SWM Plan should be undertaken.

We recommended that management prepare annually the LDRRMFIP in the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. The LDRRMFIP shall include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

discrepancies in their records as regards the Land account. Adjust and/or
correct the record that needs adjustments to fairly present the said account in

a) Create an Inventory Committee to undertake actual physical inventory of all
properties of the Municipal Government and prepare the corresponding

and submit copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office as well as to the

a) The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate with each

We have recommended Management to comply with the requirements of
COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to ensure the establishment, handling and
utilization of the petty cash fund.

We have recommended management to ensure strict observance of the
pertinent provisions of Section 7 of the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act 9184 with a view of attaining efficiency, economy
and effectiveness in its procurement opportunities.

a) Procure common-use supplies at the PS-DBM pursuant to Sections 1
and 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 to be
guaranteed that the cost of procured items are reasonable and
advantageous to management;
b) Other modes of procurement may be resorted to pursuant to the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 if the
required stocks are not available at the PS.

We have recommended management to refrain from allowing
reimbursements of expenses unless made under extreme emergency and urgent
operating requirements that do not involve huge sums of money. Also, municipal
personnel concerned comply with the requirement of adequate procurement
planning in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 which will regulate
procurement activities and discourage reimbursement of expenses.

We have recommended that management adhere to the aforementioned
provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act
No. 9184.

a) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly evaluate the

bidder, provided, that all documentary requirements are complied with; and

certifying the disbursement vouchers as to the completeness of
supporting documents in line with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to avoid
doubts on the legality, validity and propriety of transactions.
